prompt: An I²C sensor works on the bench jig but returns nothing on the new board. The only schematic change: two resistors were removed. Why does the bus fail?
snippet:
3V3 ─────────────── SDA ── MCU
                    SCL ── MCU

  [SENSOR] on SDA/SCL — no resistors to 3V3
choices: I²C lines need pull-up resistors to idle high
The sensor is missing a decoupling capacitor
The MCU pins must be configured push-pull
reveal: I²C is open-drain: devices can only pull the line low, never drive it high. Without pull-ups to Vcc the lines never return high, so there are no valid logic levels and the bus is stuck.
lesson: Open-drain buses (I²C, 1-Wire, SMBus) require pull-ups to set the high state. ~4.7 kΩ to Vcc is the common default; go lower for fast-mode or long/capacitive traces, higher to save power.
caption: Works on the jig, dead on the board. Two missing parts.
